title: primula villosa var. viscosa
intro: Primula villosa var. viscosa, also known as sticky primrose, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Primulaceae family and is native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Primula villosa var. viscosa include its ability to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
